Title: Verify that PII is not visible directly on the map canvas outside of hover/click interactions
Preconditions: User is logged in as Dispatcher with PII view permission - Multiple vehicles and passenger markers are visible on the live map - No marker is currently hovered or clicked
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal with PII view permission - 2- Navigate to the live map - 3- Observe all visible map elements (vehicle markers - passenger markers - labels - tooltips) without hovering or clicking any marker - 4- Inspect whether any PII (e.g. passenger name - address - phone number) is displayed directly on the map canvas
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Live map is displayed - 3- Map shows vehicle markers and passenger markers with no hover or click active - 4- No PII is visible directly on the map canvas - markers show only icons or non-PII indicators (e.g. vehicle ID or count badge)

Title: Verify that PII is displayed within hover popups only when the user has PII view permission
Preconditions: User is logged in with a role that has PII display permission enabled - A passenger marker is visible on the live map
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal with PII view permission - 2- Navigate to the live map - 3- Hover over a passenger marker - 4- Observe the popup content for PII (e.g. passenger name - address or contact details)
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Live map is displayed - 3- Hover popup appears over the passenger marker - 4- PII fields (such as passenger name and contact details) are visible in the hover popup - PII is limited to the popup and not shown on the main map canvas

Title: Verify that PII is displayed within click popups only when the user has PII view permission
Preconditions: User is logged in with a role that has PII display permission enabled - A passenger marker is visible on the live map
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal with PII view permission - 2- Navigate to the live map - 3- Click on a passenger marker - 4- Observe the popup content for PII (e.g. passenger name - address - date of birth)
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Live map is displayed - 3- Click popup appears - 4- PII fields are visible in the click popup - PII remains confined to the popup panel and is not surfaced on the map canvas itself

Title: Verify that PII is NOT displayed in hover popups when the user does NOT have PII view permission
Preconditions: User is logged in with a role that does NOT have PII display permission - A passenger marker is visible on the live map
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal with a role lacking PII permission - 2- Navigate to the live map - 3- Hover over a passenger marker - 4- Observe the popup content
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads with restricted role - 2- Live map is displayed - 3- Hover popup appears - 4- PII fields (name - address - contact details) are NOT shown in the popup - the popup shows only non-PII data or a permission-restricted placeholder - no PII leakage occurs

Title: Verify that PII is NOT displayed in click popups when the user does NOT have PII view permission
Preconditions: User is logged in with a role that does NOT have PII display permission - A passenger marker is visible on the live map
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal with a role lacking PII permission - 2- Navigate to the live map - 3- Click on a passenger marker - 4- Observe the click popup content
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads with restricted role - 2- Live map is displayed - 3- Click popup appears - 4- PII fields are NOT visible - popup shows only permitted non-PII information or a restricted access message - no PII is exposed

Title: Verify that passenger name is not rendered as a label or overlay on the map canvas at any zoom level
Preconditions: User is logged in as Dispatcher with PII view permission - Multiple passenger markers are displayed on the map - User zooms in to maximum zoom level
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Navigate to the live map - 3- Zoom in to maximum zoom level on a cluster of passenger markers - 4- Observe whether any passenger names or PII text appear as map labels or overlays
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Live map is displayed - 3- Map is zoomed to maximum level - 4- No passenger names or PII text are rendered as map labels or canvas overlays at any zoom level - markers remain as icons only

Title: Verify that passenger address is not visible on the map canvas even when the marker is in a zoomed-in detailed view
Preconditions: User is logged in with PII view permission - A passenger pickup or drop-off marker is displayed on the map - Map is zoomed in to show the stop location
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Navigate to the live map - 3- Zoom in to show a passenger stop marker at a detailed street level - 4- Observe whether the passenger's address or any PII text is rendered near or on the marker
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Live map is displayed - 3- Map is zoomed to street level around a passenger stop - 4- No passenger address or PII is visible as a map annotation or canvas text - stop marker shows only icon or generic stop label

Title: Verify that closing a hover or click popup immediately hides all PII from view
Preconditions: User is logged in with PII view permission - A hover or click popup is open showing PII content - User performs an action to close the popup
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Navigate to the live map - 3- Click a passenger marker to open the click popup with PII - 4- Close the popup by clicking elsewhere on the map or pressing Escape - 5- Observe whether PII remains visible anywhere on the map
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Live map is displayed - 3- Click popup is open with PII visible - 4- Popup is closed - 5- All PII immediately disappears from the screen - no PII residue or overlay remains on the map canvas

Title: Verify that PII is not exposed via browser developer tools or page source on the map canvas (no PII in DOM outside of popup)
Preconditions: User is logged in with PII view permission - The live map is open with no popup active - Browser developer tools are accessible
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Navigate to the live map with no popup open - 3- Open browser developer tools and inspect the map canvas DOM elements - 4- Search the DOM for PII such as passenger names or addresses that are rendered in the canvas but not visible to the user
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Live map is displayed - 3- Developer tools opened and DOM is inspected - 4- No PII text is present in the rendered DOM for map canvas elements when no popup is active - PII is only injected into the DOM when a hover or click popup is open

Title: Verify that PII display in hover/click popups is controlled by the "PII display in map popups - Allowed" permission setting
Preconditions: User is logged in as System Administrator - RBAC configuration panel is accessible - Two test roles exist: one with PII popup permission enabled and one with it disabled
Steps(Step): 1- Log in as System Administrator - 2- Navigate to the RBAC configuration panel - 3- Verify the "PII display in map popups" permission is togglable per role - 4- Enable PII permission for Role A and disable for Role B - 5- Log in as Role A and hover over a passenger marker - verify PII is visible - 6- Log in as Role B and hover over the same passenger marker - verify PII is not visible
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Admin portal loads - 2- RBAC configuration is accessible - 3- PII popup permission is configurable per role - 4- Roles are configured correctly - 5- Role A sees PII in hover popup - 6- Role B does NOT see PII in hover popup - permission control works correctly

Title: Verify PII visibility behavior when a user's PII permission is revoked during an active session
Preconditions: User is logged in with PII view permission - A click popup with PII is currently open - An administrator revokes the user's PII permission mid-session
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal with PII view permission - 2- Open a click popup on the live map showing PII - 3- In a separate admin session revoke the PII permission for this user - 4- Without logging out attempt to open another popup or refresh the map view - 5- Observe whether PII is still visible
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads with PII permission - 2- Click popup shows PII - 3- Permission is revoked by admin - 4- Next popup open or map refresh enforces the updated permission - 5- PII is no longer shown in subsequent popups - access control is enforced dynamically

Title: Verify that PII is not visible on the map when the application is captured via screenshot or screen share tool while no popup is active
Preconditions: User is logged in with PII view permission - Live map is open with no popup active - A screen capture is taken of the map view
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Navigate to the live map - 3- Ensure no popup is open - 4- Take a screenshot of the current map state - 5- Inspect the screenshot for any PII content
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Live map is open with no active popup - 3- Screenshot is captured - 4- No PII (names - addresses - contact info) is visible in the screenshot of the map canvas - only non-PII markers and icons are captured

Title: Verify that PII in the hover popup is not retained or re-displayed when the mouse moves away and then returns to the same marker
Preconditions: User is logged in with PII view permission - A hover popup was previously opened and the mouse moved away causing it to close
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Navigate to the live map - 3- Hover over a passenger marker - PII is shown in popup - 4- Move the mouse away - popup closes - 5- Return the mouse to the same marker - 6- Observe whether the popup re-renders with a fresh permission check or shows cached PII
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Live map is displayed - 3- Hover popup shows PII - 4- Popup closes when mouse moves away - 5- On return hover popup opens again with PII - 6- A fresh permission check is applied - if permission is still valid PII is shown; if permission was revoked PII is not shown

Title: Verify that PII popup content is not logged or exposed in browser network requests to unauthorized parties
Preconditions: User is logged in with PII view permission - A click popup is opened showing PII - Browser network tab is monitored
Steps(Step): 1- Log in to the dispatcher portal - 2- Open the browser developer tools and switch to the Network tab - 3- Navigate to the live map and click a passenger marker to open the PII popup - 4- Inspect network requests made when the popup opens - 5- Verify PII data is fetched via authenticated and secured API calls only
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Dispatcher portal loads - 2- Developer tools network tab is open - 3- Popup is opened - 4- Network requests for PII data use authenticated endpoints (e.g. include auth token) - 5- PII is not exposed via unprotected or public API calls - all PII requests return 401 or 403 for unauthenticated requests

Title: Verify PII rules apply consistently across desktop and tablet screen sizes for map hover/click popups
Preconditions: User is logged in with PII view permission - Application is accessed on desktop and tablet viewports - A passenger marker is visible on the map
Steps(Step): 1- Log in on a desktop browser - navigate to the live map - hover and click on a passenger marker - verify PII visibility behavior - 2- Repeat on a tablet-sized viewport - 3- Compare PII visibility and popup behavior across both screen sizes
Steps(Expected Result): 1- Desktop view: PII is visible only in hover/click popup - not on map canvas - popup layout is correct - 2- Tablet view: PII is visible only in hover/click popup - not on map canvas - popup adapts responsively - 3- PII containment within popup is consistent across both screen sizes
